response: In C# it is possible to measure the time it takes to send a UDP packet to a server but not the round trip time like ping. To do this you can record the time before sending the packet and then calculate the difference between the send time and the time you expect the packet to be received. However UDP is connectionless and does not guarantee delivery or provide feedback on when the packet was received. Therefore you can only measure the time it takes to send the packet from your machine not the time it takes for the packet to reach the server. You can use the System.Diagnostics.Stopwatch class to measure the time taken for the send operation. This will give you an idea of how long it took for your system to send the packet but not how long it took for the packet to arrive at the server.
